Methodology Equations

1. Normalized Difference Vegetation Index (Satellite):

$$ NDVI = \frac{NIR - Red}{NIR + Red} = \frac{B8 - B4}{B8 + B4} $$

2. Point-Level Green View Index (Street View):

$$ GVI_{current} = \frac{Area_{veg}}{Area_{total}} \times 100 $$

Where \( Area_{veg} \) is the number of vegetation pixels, and \( Area_{total} \) is the valid panorama area (excluding masked letterboxing).

3. Standardized sGVI Metric (Selected Segment):

$$ GVI_{total} = \frac{\sum_{j=1}^{8} Area_{veg, j}}{\sum_{j=1}^{8} Area_{total, j}} \times 100 $$

Where \( GVI_{total} \) represents the average Green View Index across all 8 azimuthal frames (\( j=1..8 \)).

$$ W_i = \frac{l_j}{L_{network}} = \frac{ l_j }{121642.45} $$

Where \( W_i \) is the Road Weight coefficient, \( l_j \) is the exact length of the road segment within the Voronoi polygon around point \( i \), and \( L_{network} \) is the total length of the entire road network in the analyzed urban area (calculated as 12272.6m via MST routing).

$$ sGVI_i = GVI_{total} \times W_i $$

Where \( sGVI_i \) denotes the standardized index for spatial point \( i \).

4. Actual Evapotranspiration (AET) via FAO-56 Penman-Monteith:

Loading weather data from Open-Meteo API...
$$ PET = \frac{0.408\Delta(R_n - G) + \gamma \frac{900}{T+273} u_2 (e_s - e_a)}{\Delta + \gamma(1 + 0.34u_2)} $$

Where \( PET \) is the reference evapotranspiration (mm day⁻¹); \( R_n \) is the net radiation (MJ m⁻² day⁻¹); \( G \) is the soil heat flux density (MJ m⁻² day⁻¹); \( T \) is the mean daily air temperature at 2 m (°C); \( u_2 \) is the wind speed at 2 m (m s⁻¹); \( e_s \) and \( e_a \) are the saturation and actual vapour pressure (kPa); \( \Delta \) is the slope of the vapour pressure curve (kPa °C⁻¹); and \( \gamma \) is the psychrometric constant (kPa °C⁻¹), estimated from daily surface pressure. Weather data is dynamically fetched for Angren via the Open-Meteo API for the selected date.

$$ AET = PET \times \frac{S_{RZ}}{Sr_{max}} $$

Where \( S_{RZ} \) and \( S_{rmax} \) are the storage and maximum capacity of the root zone, respectively; \( PET \) is the reference potential evapotranspiration. The root zone storage capacity ratio \( \frac{S_{RZ}}{Sr_{max}} \) is approximated by the normalized Green View Index (\( \frac{GVI_{total}}{100} \)) acting as a proxy for vegetation biomass and root development.

Model Specifications & Dataset Metrics

Semantic Segmentation Architecture SegFormer-B5 (Transformer-based, Xie et al., 2021)
Pre-trained Weights (Domain) Cityscapes 1024x1024 (Zero-Shot Transfer to Ahangaran)
Target Detection Class Vegetation / Greenery (Class ID: 8)
Baseline Model Accuracy (mIoU) 84.0%
Total Processed Panoramas 9,376 images (8 azimuthal frames per audit point)
Total Audit Points (Voronoi Polygons) 1,172 localized points
Hardware Inference Platform 12th Gen Intel Core i5-12500H, NVIDIA GeForce RTX 4060 Laptop GPU

Descriptive Statistics & Linear Regression (N=1172)

Metric Mean Std Dev Min Max
Green View Index (GVI %) 24.00 11.47 3.73 63.51
Satellite NDVI (Voronoi 30m) 0.2943 0.1020 0.0499 0.6019
Metric Pearson Correlation (r) Spearman Rank (ρ) Sample Size (N)
GVI vs. NDVI (30m Voronoi) 0.477 (p < 0.001) 0.479 (p < 0.001) 1172 points
